name: Claude Fable 5
archDisplay: Dense transformer, architecture undisclosed; always-on adaptive thinking, effort-parameter controlled
note: Model id claude-fable-5. This is the correct current name of the family the task flagged as 'a newer Claude model family' — it is Anthropic's first widely-released 'Mythos-class' model (a tier above Opus), positioned above Opus 4.8. It shares identical underlying weights with the invitation-only Claude Mythos 5 (claude-mythos-5, Project Glasswing only) but adds safety classifiers that can refuse requests; Mythos 5 does not have those classifiers. Notably, access to Fable 5 was briefly restricted/pulled and then restored in July 2026 following a jailbreak-linked export-control issue (see anthropic.com/news/redeploying-fable-5) — this does not change its specs. NO SEPARATE '(High)' PRODUCT: like Sonnet 5, 'high' is the documented default effort level and is not a distinct marketed SKU; not added as a sibling entry. Claude Mythos 5 itself was NOT added as a separate corpus entry given its restricted, non-public availability and the difficulty independently sourcing benchmark numbers specific to it (as opposed to Fable 5).

results.notes: Ahead of Opus 4.8 (69.2%) and GPT-5.5 (58.6%). [score corrected during round-5 full-corpus accuracy re-verify: 80.3 -> 80]
SWE-bench Verified, Vals AI independent leaderboard re-run, updated 2026-07-09. Leads the leaderboard.

GDPval-AA (economically-valuable real-world professional work), from Anthropic's official benchmark-comparison chart on the announcement page (ahead of Opus 4.8's 1890, GPT-5.5's 1769, Gemini 3.1 Pro's 1314). Independently corroborated by a third-party benchmark explainer (Silicon Report) citing the identical 1932 figure.
OSWorld-Verified (computer use), from the same official comparison chart (ahead of Opus 4.8's 83.4%; Claude Mythos Preview scored marginally higher at 85.4% but that is a different, earlier model).

From a separate 'Offensive cyber evaluations' chart on the same announcement page that explicitly plots a 'Claude Fable' series (distinct from Opus 4.8, Mythos Preview, and Mythos 5) at 0.0% on CyberGym as well as on Firefox/OSS-Fuzz/CyScenarioBench. This reflects Fable 5's built-in safety refusal/fallback behavior on offensive-cyber tasks by design (per the model's safeguards described in its system card), not a raw capability ceiling — flagging this clearly since it could otherwise read as a capability score.

From the official Claude Fable 5 / Claude Mythos 5 System Card, Section 8.1 'Evaluation summary' master table (Fable 5 column specifically, distinct from Mythos 5's 88.0 in the same row). Extracted via pdftotext -layout from the primary system-card PDF.

GPQA Diamond, Artificial Analysis's independent evaluation (not an Anthropic-published figure -- confirmed by directly fetching both official Anthropic launch pages, anthropic.com/news/claude-fable-5-mythos-5 and the platform.claude.com docs page, and searching each specifically for 'GPQA'/'GPQA Diamond': zero mentions on either). Cross-corroborated at the identical 92.6% by a second independent aggregator (benchlm.ai/models/claude-fable, which likewise attributes it to 'Artificial Analysis') and by a July 2, 2026 GPQA leaderboard snapshot placing Fable 5 at 92.6%, just behind MiniMax M3 (92.9%) and ahead of the pack. One lower-quality secondary roundup (a Zvi Mowshowitz Substack post) cited a rounder '94%, described as saturated' figure without clear sourcing; given the 92.6% figure's triple corroboration from sources that explicitly cite Artificial Analysis as the measuring party, 92.6% was used as the more reliable number and the 94% figure was not added as a second contested entry.

Humanity's Last Exam, Artificial Analysis's own independent evaluation as part of their Intelligence Index v4.1 methodology (AA's own article states in prose: 'Claude Fable 5 scores 53% on Humanity's Last Exam'; a secondary aggregator relaying the same AA measurement gives the more precise 53.3%, used here). This is explicitly NOT an Anthropic-published number: Anthropic's own launch page and docs page were both fetched directly and searched for 'HLE'/'Humanity's Last Exam' with zero hits, and a dedicated third-party analysis (espressio.ai) states outright that 'Claude Fable 5 publishes capability claims rather than HLE or GPQA numbers' and that Anthropic has 'retired GPQA Diamond and AIME from its reporting entirely, declaring them saturated.' A separate aggregator (digitalapplied.com) claimed a materially different 64.5% 'HLE with tools' figure attributed to an 'Anthropic benchmark table' -- this was deliberately excluded since it directly contradicts the more corroborated finding (from two independent sources) that Anthropic never published an HLE number for this model at all, making that specific attribution unreliable.

Text Arena (LMArena/Arena.ai) crowd-sourced Elo rating, confirmed directly on the live arena.ai/leaderboard page: 1505 +/- 8, rank #1 overall in the text arena as of the July 10, 2026 snapshot (also #1 in the 'Expert' and 'Math' subcategories). Cross-corroborated by an independent secondary write-up (quasa.io) citing the identical 1505 +/- 8 figure from 7,252 votes. Note for context (not added as a separate figure since it is the same underlying model/leaderboard, just an earlier point in time): Fable 5 briefly peaked near ~1525 Elo immediately after its June 9, 2026 launch before a June 12-13, 2026 US export-control-driven worldwide suspension of non-US access disrupted voting volume; access was restored July 1, 2026, and the July 10 snapshot value used here reflects the stabilized post-restoration rating.

Agents' Last Exam (ALE-CLI, Overall leaderboard), Claude Code harness, XHigh reasoning effort: Pass rate 25.7% / Score 48.7% (rank 12 overall). Score component used per corpus convention. Mirrored from agents-last-exam.org/leaderboard. [score corrected during adversarial verify: proposed 48.7 -> 48.5, per verifier: Verified by fetching the raw source HTML (curl, not the summarizing WebFetch tool, which gave inconsistent numbers across repeated calls on the same table). The source page (snorkel.ai/leaderboard/agents-last-exam) embeds several DISTINCT leaderboard splits in its JSON payload: "overall" (label "Overall", 50 entries) and "ale_cli" (label "ALE-CLI", description "linux task only", 55 entries) are separate tables, not the same thing. For harness=Claude Code, model=claude-fable-5, effort=XHigh, there are two different rows, one per split: - "Overall" split: rank 12, pass_rate 25.7%, score 48.7% - "ALE-CLI" split: rank 13, pass_rate 23.8%, score 48.5% The claim's notes explicitly say "Agents' Last Exam (ALE-CLI, Overall leaderboard)" and give pass rate 25.7% / score 48.7% / rank 12 — but those three numbers are actually the "Overall" split's row, not the "ALE-CLI" split's row. This is exactly the wrong-benchmark-variant pattern flagged in the task instructions (and matches this project's known variant-conflation failure mode from the prior dataset audit): the notes cite "ALE-CLI" by name, but the number pulled belongs to the sibling "Overall" split. The true ALE-CLI-split number for this exact model/harness/effort combination is pass rate 23.8%, score 48.5%, rank 13. Model, harness, and effort level are otherwise correctly identified (Claude Code harness, XHigh effort, claude-fable-5 is real and distinct from claude-opus-4-8 which has a similar 25.7% pass rate on the Overall split — likely the actual source of the mixup, since Opus 4-8's Overall pass rate of 25.7% appears to have been attached to Fable 5's ALE-CLI-labeled entry). Recommend correcting the score to 48.5 (and pass rate/rank to 23.8%/13 if those fields are tracked) if the corpus intends the ALE-CLI-specific split, or alternatively relabeling the notes to say "Overall leaderboard" (dropping the ALE-CLI qualifier) if 48.7 is the number actually wanted — but as currently worded, the notes and the number don't match the same split.]

Vals AI independent eval. MMLU-Pro 91.502% (ranked 1st of 120 models per the page), from the model page's embedded score bars (raw HTML fetch, cross-checked against the WebFetch-summarized ranking text). Not previously tracked for this model.

Vals AI independent eval. LiveCodeBench 89.778% (ranked 1st of 127 models per the page), from the model page's embedded score data. Corroborated by a separate morphllm.com roundup citing the identical 89.78% figure and #1 rank. Not previously tracked for this model.

Vals AI independent eval. MMMU 89.306% (ranked 1st of 82 models per the page), from the model page's embedded score bars (raw HTML fetch). Not previously tracked for this model — Anthropic's own launch materials for Fable 5 did not appear to publish an MMMU figure.

verificationNotes:
Model id, pricing, context window, and Fable-vs-Mythos relationship confirmed directly from Anthropic's own Claude Platform docs (fetched in full). The July 2026 access-restoration event was corroborated by an independent news report (TheHackerNews) as well as Anthropic's own 'Redeploying Claude Fable 5' post referenced in the docs page.

2026-07-16 gap-resolution pass (mmlu, math, aime, humaneval): checked all three required source tiers and found no credible published score for Claude Fable 5 on any of the four. (1) Own official materials -- the launch docs page (platform.claude.com/docs/.../introducing-claude-fable-5-and-claude-mythos-5, fetched in full) contains zero mentions of MMLU, MATH, AIME, or HumanEval. The official Fable 5/Mythos 5 System Card (www-cdn.anthropic.com/d00db56fa754a1b115b6dd7cb2e3c342ee809620.pdf, downloaded and searched via pdftotext -layout) likewise has no score for any of the four anywhere in its ~10,600 lines -- its master evaluation table (Section 8.1, Table 8.1.A: SWE-bench Pro/Verified, Terminal-Bench 2.1, BrowseComp, HLE, CharXiv, BioMysteryBench, OSWorld-Verified, CritPt, ArxivMath, RiemannBench, GraphWalks, FrontierCode, GDPval-AA, GDP.pdf, OfficeQA Pro, AutomationBench, Blueprint-Bench 2, Legal Agent Benchmark, HealthBench) omits all four target benchmarks, and Section 8.10 (USAMO 2026) explicitly states AIME "was a popular AI benchmark last year but is now saturated," i.e. deliberate retirement, not an oversight. (2) Independent leaderboards -- Vals AI's Claude Fable 5 page (vals.ai/models/anthropic_claude-fable-5, raw HTML) lists MMLU-Pro, GPQA Diamond, LiveCodeBench, MMMU, SWE-bench, Terminal-Bench 2.1, ProofBench, IOI, LegalBench, etc. but no plain MMLU, MATH, AIME, or HumanEval entry; a secondary relay of the same Vals data (benchlm.ai) confirms this and explicitly lists "Math: Not measured." Epoch AI's model page (epoch.ai/models/claude-fable-5) exposes only the composite Epoch Capabilities Index (~160-161) and FrontierMath tier scores, again no MMLU/MATH/AIME/HumanEval. A wandb relay of the model's full benchmark set (wandb.ai/byyoung3/ml-news/reports/Claude-Fable-5-Benchmark-Scores) explicitly states "The document does not include scores for MMLU, MATH, AIME, or GSM8K benchmarks." One boilerplate aggregator page (requesty.ai) listed "MMLU Pro, ... HumanEval, MATH, AIME" in generic template prose but displayed no actual score for any of them and was disregarded as non-data. (3) Sibling cross-reference -- Claude Opus 4.8 and Claude Sonnet 5 (same current generation, same corpus) likewise carry zero recorded results for all four slugs, consistent with a family-wide move away from reporting these benchmarks. This corpus's own benchmark catalog already flags mmlu as "largely saturated at the frontier" and humaneval as "long-saturated at the frontier," matching the observed pattern. Conclusion: all four are genuine, deliberate non-publications rather than research gaps -- mmlu, math, aime, and humaneval all resolved as omitted_no_evidence, with no changes made to any existing result.

2026-07-17 second-pass gap-resolution round (mmlu, math, aime, humaneval) — new source types only, per round-2 instructions. Genuinely checked all 6 recommended new source categories (not just the 3-minimum bar):

(1) Stanford HELM — fetched each HELM leaderboard project's actual data directly (not just the SPA shell): classic (release v0.4.0), lite (v1.13.0), mmlu-specific project (v1.13.0), and capabilities (v1.15.0), pulling the real groups.json/group-table JSON from storage.googleapis.com/crfm-helm-public/{project}/benchmark_output/releases/{release}/... and grepping every Claude model name present. Result: HELM's most recent Claude model in any of these four projects is claude-sonnet-4-5-20250929 (capabilities project) — everything else tops out at claude-3-5-sonnet-20241022 or earlier. None of HELM's live projects have been updated to include any Claude 5-generation model, let alone Claude Fable 5 (released 2026-06-09). This is a hard, verifiable absence (not a "didn't look hard enough" gap) — HELM simply has not run this model.

(2) Academic papers (arXiv / Semantic Scholar) — queried the Semantic Scholar Graph API directly (api.semanticscholar.org) for "Claude Fable 5" and confirmed several genuine 2026 papers use it as a baseline/subject: "Capabilities of Claude Fable 5 on Biomedical Challenge Problems" (arXiv 2607.10849, fetched in full — 8 biomedical benchmarks only, explicitly no MMLU/MATH/AIME/HumanEval), "WorkBench Revisited: Workplace Agents Two Years On" (arXiv 2606.13715 — reports 98% WorkBench completion, not a standard-benchmark number), "LoSoNA" (arXiv 2606.14600 — reports 81.6% on a bespoke social-norm benchmark), "A Red-Team Study of Anthropic Fable 5 & Opus 4.8 Models" (arXiv 2606.18193 — jailbreak-success rates only), and a catastrophic-forgetting comparative study (arXiv 2601.18699) that uses Fable 5 only for qualitative drift analysis. None report MMLU, MATH, AIME, or HumanEval scores for Fable 5. A claimed "arXiv 2605.14153 system card" lead (surfaced by a search-engine summary) was checked and is a hallucinated attribution — that ID actually belongs to an unrelated paper, "ExploitBench: A Capability Ladder Benchmark for LLM Cybersecurity Agents" (which also predates Fable 5's release and does not evaluate it).

(3) GitHub eval-harness run logs — ran `gh api search/code` for `"claude-fable-5"` combined with MMLU and `filename:results.json` (329 and 61 hits respectively) and inspected the most plausible candidates. Findings: (a) a genuine lm-evaluation-harness / vLLM-style results.json exists (XuehaoSun/lb_eval, results/empero-ai/Qwable-9B-Claude-Fable-5-AutoRound-W4A16-RTN/results_2026-06-27...) but it evaluates "Qwable-9B", a small open-weight model distilled/post-trained on Claude Fable 5's outputs — not Fable 5 itself via API — so its MMLU number is not attributable to Fable 5. Same pattern found for a sibling "Qwythos" distillation. (b) A gap-tracking meta-repo (christicode/benchmark-citations, GAPS.md) independently lists the exact same ~39 benchmark names from the official system card's Section 8.1 master table (ArxivMath, AutomationBench, BrowseComp, CritPt, RiemannBench, USAMO, etc.) as "already have scores" — MMLU/MATH/AIME/HumanEval are conspicuously absent from that list too, an independent cross-check confirming the system card genuinely omits them. (c) Several other results.json / results.jsonl files found via code search (Sreenivas-Sadhu-Prabhakara/claude-experimentation-guide, exmergo/dex, justsml/ExploitHunter.app, etc.) are proprietary custom-task evals (production-codebase debugging, security-capture tasks), not MMLU/MATH/AIME/HumanEval runs. No genuine harness run against the real claude-fable-5 API for any of the four target benchmarks was found.

(4) Other leaderboards/aggregators — OpenRouter's Claude Fable 5 page (openrouter.ai/anthropic/claude-fable-5) surfaces only Terminal-Bench 2.1 (84.3%) and OSWorld-Verified (85.0%), both already recorded; no MMLU/MATH/AIME/HumanEval. LiveBench.ai's homepage returned an empty/non-rendering response (JS-only SPA with no server-side content reachable via curl) and no search result surfaced a Fable 5-specific LiveBench score. Scale AI's SEAL leaderboard: confirmed via search that "Claude Fable 5 is not yet listed" on SEAL as of the check date (its most recent Claude entry is Opus 4.6-thinking). PapersWithCode's MMLU SOTA leaderboard page (paperswithcode.com/sota/multi-task-language-understanding-on-mmlu) was fetched directly and grepped for "fable" — zero matches; PapersWithCode's MMLU board is a community-submission board and does not carry 2026 proprietary frontier-model rows.

(5) Non-English-language sources — ran targeted Korean, Chinese, and Japanese searches. Chinese tech-media coverage (36kr.com review, fetched and checked directly) explicitly states its benchmark chart section has no extractable standardized numbers ("基准测试跑分图如下" with only a qualitative "commanding lead" claim); qbitai/zhihu coverage of the sibling Mythos 5 launion likewise centers on qualitative claims (code-generation feats) not MMLU/MATH/AIME/HumanEval. datalearner.com (a Chinese aggregator that often does carry structured benchmark tables) was unreachable from this environment (DNS resolution failure on both WebFetch and curl) so could not be directly verified either way. Korean and Japanese searches surfaced no independent regional-language testing; they either repeated the already-recorded Vals AI MMLU-Pro 91.5% figure or funneled back to a single English-language aggregator, automatio.ai/models/claude-fable-5. That page was fetched and its raw HTML/JSON-LD schema.org markup inspected directly: it lists "MMLU": "91.2%", "MMLU Pro": "82%", "AIME 2025": "90%", "HumanEval": "93.5%" with the text explicitly per-field disclosing "No attribution to external source provided." The MMLU-Pro figure it gives (82%) directly conflicts with the well-corroborated, independently-verified 91.5% already in this corpus (Vals AI, triple-corroborated) — a strong internal-consistency failure indicating this page is templated/auto-generated filler content, not a real measurement. This was deliberately investigated and rejected as non-credible per the "never invent or estimate a score" rule, rather than added.

(6) Sibling-model / release-wave comparison tables — checked Claude Mythos 5 / Project Glasswing coverage (multiple aggregators) and a same-wave competitor-model wave (GLM-5.2, launched days after Fable 5, whose comparison articles were checked since they often carry baseline tables) — none report MMLU/MATH/AIME/HumanEval numbers for Fable 5; comparisons stay confined to SWE-bench Pro, Terminal-Bench, and Design-Arena Elo. A broad multi-model comparison table (iternal.ai/llm-selection-guide, fetched directly, Section 2.2) lists Fable 5's GPQA Diamond (92.6%), MMLU-Pro (91.5%), SWE-bench Verified (95.0%), HLE (53.3%), and Arena Elo (~1665) — all already recorded in this corpus — and explicitly states elsewhere in the same document that Anthropic did not publish GPQA/MMLU-Pro/AIME-type academic benchmarks for the Sonnet 5 sibling either, corroborating a family-wide reporting gap rather than a Fable-5-specific one.

Conclusion: across all 6 new source types (HELM, arXiv/Semantic Scholar, GitHub eval logs, other leaderboards, non-English sources, sibling/release-wave tables), no credible independent measurement of Claude Fable 5 on MMLU, MATH, AIME, or HumanEval exists as of 2026-07-17. The one source that superficially appeared to have numbers (automatio.ai) was directly inspected and found to be uncredited, internally-inconsistent template content, and was explicitly rejected rather than used. All four benchmarks remain omitted_no_evidence; no changes made to the existing results array.
